word_list.word: nothing so bold as a blind mare
word_list.phonetic_text: nə.θɪng soʊ boʊld æz ə blaɪnd mer
word_list.phonetic_am_text: nē.thing sooo booold āz ē blaind mer
word_list.pronunciation: nˈʌθɪŋ sˌoʊ bˈoʊld æz ɐ blˈaɪnd mˈɛɹ

senses.definition: used to imply that when someone is ignorant of the dangers involved in a situation, they may act more boldly or recklessly than someone who is aware of the risks

word_list.word: there (are|is) none so deaf as those (that|who) will not hear
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɑːɹ ɪz nˈʌn sˌoʊ dˈɛf æz ðoʊz ðæt hˌuː wɪl nˌɑːt hˈɪɹ

senses.definition: used to suggest that some people are so stubborn and resistant to new ideas or information that they refuse to listen or pay attention, even if it is in their best interest

word_list.word: what the eye does not see, the heart does not grieve over
word_list.pronunciation: wˌʌt ðɪ ˈaɪ dʌznˌɑːt sˈiː ðə hˈɑːɹt dʌznˌɑːt ɡɹˈiːv ˈoʊvɚ

senses.definition: used to imply that people may choose to remain ignorant of certain things to protect themselves from potential emotional distress

word_list.word: admiration is the daughter of ignorance
word_list.pronunciation: ˌædmɚɹˈeɪʃən ɪz ðə dˈɔːɾɚɹ ʌv ˈɪɡnɚɹəns

senses.definition: used to suggest that when someone lacks knowledge or understanding about a certain subject, they tend to have a heightened admiration or reverence for it

word_list.word: frog in the well knows nothing of the sea
word_list.pronunciation: fɹˈɑːɡ ɪnðə wˈɛl nˈoʊz nˈʌθɪŋ ʌvðə sˈiː

senses.definition: used to imply that someone with a narrow-minded perspective can never fully understand the vastness and diversity of the world beyond their limited environment or worldview
used to imply that ignorance is a result of one's own choices or lack of effort to gain knowledge and understanding, and that it can lead to negative consequences
used to suggest that not knowing certain information can be preferable to facing it, particularly when it is unpleasant or burdensome

word_list.word: there (are|is) none so blind as those (that|who) will not see
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɑːɹ ɪz nˈʌn sˌoʊ blˈaɪnd æz ðoʊz ðæt hˌuː wɪl nˌɑːt sˈiː

senses.definition: used to imply that some people choose to ignore the truth and refuse to see what contradicts their beliefs

word_list.word: men are blind in their own cause
word_list.pronunciation: mˈɛn ɑːɹ blˈaɪnd ɪn ðɛɹ ˈoʊn kˈɔːz

senses.definition: used to imply that people may be unable to see the flaws or potential dangers in something they strongly believe in, due to their emotional investment in it

word_list.word: half the world knows not how the other half lives
word_list.pronunciation: hˈæf ðə wˈɜːld nˈoʊz nˌɑːt hˌaʊ ðɪ ˈʌðɚ hˈæf lˈaɪvz

senses.definition: used to suggest that people often lack knowledge or understanding of the lives of others, especially when their lifestyles, experiences, and perspectives are very different
